title: Eragrostis damiensiana
taxonomy: Eragrostis damiensiana is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. This species belongs to the genus Eragrostis, which consists of over 250 species of grasses. The genus Eragrostis is further classified under the subfamily Chloridoideae. Within the Chloridoideae subfamily, Eragrostis damiensiana is placed in the tribe Cynodonteae. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and genetic characteristics.

intro: Eragrostis damiensiana, also known as Panic grass, is a perennial grass native to tropical and subtropical regions. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions due to its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Eragrostis damiensiana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat conditions such as coughs, colds, and skin irritations.
